title: Luzula campestris sudetica
taxonomy: Luzula campestris sudetica is classified as a subspecies of Luzula campestris, a species of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. This classification is based on its geographical distribution and distinct characteristics. Luzula campestris sudetica is native to Europe and is often found in wet meadows and along streams. As a subspecies, it shares many characteristics with Luzula campestris but has distinct features that set it apart. The classification of Luzula campestris sudetica is used to distinguish it from other similar species within the Juncaceae family.
uses: Luzula campestris sudetica, also known as the alpine woodrush, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant contains compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. In folk remedies, the roots and leaves of Luzula campestris sudetica have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
